questions.statement:
<p>The sentence below has been divided into three parts. Select the part of the sentence that has an error. If the sentence has no error, select the option &#39;No Error&#39;.</p>

<p>The developing countries began/ to copy and re-create aspects of/ the developed countries economies.</p>
options: to copy and re-create aspects of /

questions.statement:
<p><span style="font-weight: 700;">Direction: Select the segment of the sentence that contains the grammatical error. If there is no error, mark &#39;No error&#39; as your answer.</span></p>

<p>This news (A)/ were&nbsp;of little consolation (B)/ to us (C). No error(D)</p>

<p>Any part of the following sentence may have error. Find out which part of the sentence has an error or no error.</p>

<p>It was him / who came running / into the&nbsp;classroom.&nbsp;</p>

<p><span style="font-weight: 700; text-align: justify;">Directions: In the following question, some part of the sentence may have errors. Find out which part of the sentence has an error and select the appropriate option. If a sentence is free from error, select &#39;No Error&#39;.</span></p>

<p>Each of these boys(A)/ do their work(B)/ because of exam&nbsp;pressure.(C)/no error(D)</p>

<p><span style="font-weight: bolder;">Parts of the following sentence have been given as options. Select the option that contains an error.</span></p>

<div>Measles, sometimes a highly contagious and potentially serious disease, are preventable through vaccination and public health measures.</div>

<p>Parts of the following sentence are given as options. Identify the part that contains a grammatical error. If there is no error, select &#39;No error&#39;.</p>

<p>Shyam, so good with weapons, fail to find the words / to tell him that everything he&rsquo;d done / so far, all the compromises he&rsquo;d made, had been for love.</p>
options: to tell him that everything he&rsquo;d done /
Shyam, so good with weapons, fail to find the words /
so far, all the compromises he&rsquo;d made, had been for love. /

questions.statement: Select the sentence that has a grammatical error.
options: This is a very difficult task to accomplish. /
A young boy offered her his seat. /
I wish I had eraser to erase all the mistakes in my life. /
I have a lot of work to complete. /
questions.explanation:
The correct answer is '3' i.e. I wish I had eraser to erase all the mistakes in my life .

Key Points

  • The sentence "I wish I had eraser to erase all the mistakes in my life" is missing an article before "eraser".
  • The correct form should be "an eraser" because "eraser" is a singular, countable noun that requires an article.
  • In English, we use the article "an" before words that start with a vowel sound.

Correct sentence: I wish I had an eraser to erase all the mistakes in my life.

Additional Information

  • Usage of Articles:
    • "a": Used before words that begin with a consonant sound (e.g., a car, a book).
    • "an": Used before words that begin with a vowel sound (e.g., an apple, an idea).
    • "the": Used when referring to something specific or previously mentioned (e.g., the dog, the answer).

questions.statement:
<p><strong>The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them may contain an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options.</strong></p>

<p>The teacher suggested that he should to work harder to improve his grades.</p>

<p>Select the most appropriate option that can rectify the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to rectify it, select &lsquo;no rectification needed&rsquo;.</p>

<p>This film is a non-stop riot that many cite as one of the <u>funny</u> films of all time.</p>
